questions.prompt: A function f(x) represents the cost in dollars to produce x units of a product. If the inverse function f⁻¹(x) exists, what does f⁻¹(500) represent in this context?
options: A) The cost to produce 500 units
B) The number of units that can be produced for $500
C) The revenue generated from selling 500 units
D) The profit when 500 units are sold
questions.correctAnswer: B) The number of units that can be produced for $500
questions.explanation: An inverse function reverses the input and output of the original function. Since f(x) takes units as input and gives cost as output, f⁻¹(x) takes cost as input and gives units as output. Therefore, f⁻¹(500) means: given a cost of $500, how many units can be produced? This demonstrates the conceptual understanding that inverse functions swap the roles of independent and dependent variables.
